question: What are the EC numbers of the two most commonly used chemicals for the virus testing method in the paper about SPFMV and SPCSV in the Pearl Of Africa from 2016? Return the semicolon-separated numbers in the order of the alphabetized chemicals.

research_queries: SPFMV SPCSV Pearl of Africa 2016 research paper
Sweet potato virus Uganda 2016 virus testing chemicals
SPFMV SPCSV virus detection methods EC numbers
Pearl of Africa Uganda sweet potato virus testing 2016
Sweet Potato Feathery Mottle Virus Chlorotic Stunt Virus Uganda
working_plan: Search for the specific 2016 paper about SPFMV and SPCSV in Pearl of Africa (Uganda)
Locate and access the full paper to identify virus testing methodology section
Extract information about chemicals used for virus testing methods
Identify the two most commonly used chemicals from the paper
Research EC numbers for these identified chemicals
Alphabetize chemical names and order EC numbers accordingly
Format final answer as semicolon-separated EC numbers

solver.reasoning_summary: Found the specific 2016 paper by Tugume et al. about SPFMV and SPCSV virus testing in Uganda (Pearl of Africa). The paper describes two main virus testing methods: NCM-ELISA for SPCFV detection and TAS-ELISA for SPCSV detection. Both are enzyme-linked immunosorbent assays that commonly use alkaline phosphatase (EC 3.1.3.1) and horseradish peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.7) as the two most frequently used enzymes. Alphabetizing the chemical names: alkaline phosphatase comes before horseradish peroxidase, giving the EC numbers in order: 3.1.3.1;1.11.1.7.

issues: The reasoning doesn't clearly establish that the specific 2016 paper identifies alkaline phosphatase and horseradish peroxidase as the 'two most commonly used chemicals' for the virus testing methods. The claim appears to be based on general knowledge about ELISA assays rather than explicit content from the paper stating these are the most commonly used chemicals.

The question asks about 'the virus testing method' (singular) but the reasoning discusses two different methods (NCM-ELISA and TAS-ELISA) without clarifying how this singular reference relates to the multiple methods described.

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Searched "Pearl of Africa" on Google.
2. Noted the answer from the results.
3. Searched "SPFMV and SPCSV in Uganda 2016 paper" on Google.
4. Opened "Effects of Sweet Potato Feathery Mottle Virus and ..." at https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/jph.12451.
5. Found the section on virus testing.
6. Searched "most commonly used chemicals for ELISA" on Google.
7. Noted horseradish peroxidase and alkaline phosphatase from the results.
8. Searched "horseradish peroxidase EC number" on Google.
9. Noted the answer from the featured text snippet (1.11.1.7).
10. Searched "alkaline phosphatase EC number" on Google.
11. Noted the answer from the featured text snippet (3.1.3.1).
12. Alphabetized the chemicals.
13. Put the numbers in the order of the chemicals.
